# Mermaid Design Code — Full Context

## Overview
Design code standard for creating interactive Mermaid diagrams in HTML across 100star projects. Every diagram follows one standard: consistent fonts, colors, zoom/pan, dark/light theme, and a paired description file.

## Stack
- Mermaid v11 via CDN ESM
- Google Fonts: Sora 700 (headings), Lato 400/700 (body)
- CSS Variables for dark/light theme
- Vanilla JS zoom/pan with fit-to-view
- localStorage for theme persistence

## File Structure
Every diagram is a pair of files:
- `<name>-<YYYY-MM-DD>.html` — interactive diagram (HTML + Mermaid)
- `<name>-<YYYY-MM-DD>-description.md` — text walkthrough

## Key Rules
- Arrows: `-->` for flow, `-.-` for dependencies, never `==>`
- Node style: pastel fill + colored stroke + `color:#000`
- Services: dashed border (`stroke-dasharray:5 5`)
- Subgraphs: outer = warm yellow (#fffbeb), inner = gray (clusterBkg)
- Emoji: HTML entities only (`&#x1F3A8;`), not Unicode
- Theme: always specify themeVariables for BOTH dark and light modes

## Node Color Palette (classDef)
- Auth: fill:#eff6ff, stroke:#3b82f6
- UI: fill:#f5f3ff, stroke:#8b5cf6
- Layout: fill:#f0fdfa, stroke:#14b8a6
- Deploy: fill:#eff6ff, stroke:#3b82f6
- Service: fill:#fff, stroke:#999 (dashed)
- API: fill:#ecfeff, stroke:#06b6d4
- Repo: fill:#f8fafc, stroke:#94a3b8
- Distribution: fill:#fdf2f8, stroke:#ec4899
- Code: fill:#6366f1, stroke:#818cf8 (bold)

## Pre-save Checklist
- No `==>` arrows (use `-->`)
- No orphan nodes
- Emoji via HTML entities
- `direction` in every subgraph
- classDef assigned to every node
- Dark/light theme works
- Zoom/pan works
- Fit button works
- Creation date present

## Full Guidelines
See mermaid-guidelines.md for complete reference with code examples.
